data.title: Swissquote Group Holding (SWX:SQN) Margin Strength Reinforces Bullish Narratives In FY 2025 Results
data.description:
Swissquote Group Holding (SWX:SQN) has put up a busy FY 2025 scorecard, with second half revenue of CHF 388.1 million and basic EPS of CHF 13.88 sitting alongside trailing twelve month revenue of CHF 760.8 million and EPS of CHF 24.49, underpinned by reported earnings growth of 24.6% over the last year. The company has seen revenue move from CHF 356.9 million in the second half of 2024 to CHF 372.7 million in the first half of 2025 and CHF 388.1 million in the second half of 2025, while net income excluding extra items stepped from CHF 149.6 million to CHF 158.2 million and then CHF 208.2 million over the same periods, with trailing net profit margins now running at 48.2%. Together, these figures set the tone for a results season story built around profitability and growth potential.

See our full analysis for Swissquote Group Holding.

With the latest numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the widely followed narratives around Swissquote Group Holding and where those stories might be challenged by the data.

See what the community is saying about Swissquote Group Holding

Advertisement

TTM profit margin at 48.2%

  • Over the last 12 months, Swissquote converted CHF 366.4 million of net income from CHF 760.8 million of revenue, which works out to a 48.2% net profit margin compared with 42.8% a year earlier.
  • Supporters of the bullish narrative point to this high margin as evidence that investments in AI and platform upgrades are paying off, and the data gives them some help but not a free pass.
    • On one hand, profit growth of 24.6% over the past year and 20.9% per year over five years lines up with the idea that technology spending has not prevented Swissquote from keeping a high share of revenue as profit.
    • On the other hand, the bullish view expects margins to reach 47.4% in a few years, which is only moderately above the current 48.2% trailing figure, so future margin expansion assumptions sit on top of a level that already looks demanding.
High current margins and bullish expectations create plenty of room for debate around how much further profitability can stretch before it hits practical limits.🐂 Swissquote Group Holding Bull Case

Revenue and EPS step up across halves

  • On a half year view, revenue moved from CHF 356.9 million in 2H 2024 to CHF 372.7 million in 1H 2025 and CHF 388.1 million in 2H 2025, while net income excluding extra items went from CHF 149.6 million to CHF 158.2 million and then CHF 208.2 million, with basic EPS rising from CHF 10.01 to CHF 10.60 and then CHF 13.88.
  • Analysts with a more cautious, bearish narrative argue that rising regulatory and technology costs will eat into this earnings profile, and the recent figures give them as many questions as answers.
    • The step up in net income and EPS across the last three reported halves suggests that, so far, higher spend on compliance and cybersecurity has still sat alongside higher reported profit rather than clearly cutting into it.
    • At the same time, forecasts for earnings growth of about 14.8% per year and revenue growth of about 8.2% per year leave room for those extra costs to build over time, so bears can still point to the risk that this recent run rate does not fully reflect future expense pressure.
Bears focus on rising structural costs, but the recent half on half progression in revenue and EPS means those concerns are not yet obvious in the headline numbers.🐻 Swissquote Group Holding Bear Case

Valuation sits between peers and DCF

  • Swissquote trades on a trailing P/E of 15.7x against a peer average of 18.2x and a European Capital Markets industry average of 15x, while a DCF fair value of CHF 572.46 compares with a current share price of CHF 382.60, leaving the stock about 33.2% below that DCF level.
  • Consensus narrative views these mixed signals as a balance between solid fundamentals and market caution, and the numbers highlight why views diverge.
    • The P/E sitting below peers but slightly above the broader industry average suggests investors are prepared to pay a bit more than the industry norm, yet not as much as for closer comparables, which can be read as a measured stance rather than clear optimism or pessimism.
    • Against that, the gap between the current share price and the DCF fair value is large enough that some investors may focus more on the potential upside in that model than on the relatively modest premium to the industry P/E.

data.title: Uber Rivian Robotaxi Deal Tests Uber’s Capital Light Story
data.description:
• Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) plans to invest up to $1.25b in Rivian Automotive to deploy fully autonomous electric robotaxis.
  • The partnership is exclusive for this vehicle program, with up to 50,000 Rivian R2 robotaxis targeted to operate on Uber's platform by 2031.
  • Initial deployment is planned for 2028, with a rollout across 25 cities in the U.S., Canada, and Europe.
  • The deal marks Uber's return to owning and operating a large autonomous fleet after several years focused on an asset light model.

Uber shares recently closed at $75.34, with the stock up 3.2% over the past week and 6.8% over the past month. Over a longer horizon, NYSE:UBER has a 3 year return of 141.6% and a 5 year return of 39.8%, while year to date performance stands at a 9.1% decline and the 1 year return is 1.3%.

For investors, the Rivian robotaxi plan highlights a choice by Uber to take on more capital and operational responsibility in autonomous ride hailing. How efficiently the company manages this shift, alongside its core app based marketplace, is likely to be a key factor to watch as the 2028 launch window approaches.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Uber Technologies by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Uber Technologies.

5 things going right for Uber Technologies that this headline doesn't cover.

The Rivian deal sits at the center of Uber’s push into autonomous mobility because it blends exclusivity, capital commitment and a clear vehicle roadmap. Unlike Uber’s arrangements with NVIDIA, Zoox, Motional and Wayve, which mostly keep hardware off Uber’s balance sheet, this agreement ties Uber or its fleet partners to buying 10,000 Rivian R2 robotaxis initially, with options to scale to 50,000 units across 25 cities by 2031. That scale, if reached, could help Uber build a recognisable, app specific robotaxi fleet in key markets. Rivian in turn gains a long term anchor customer. For you as an investor, the trade off is straightforward. Uber gains more control over vehicle supply and user experience, but also takes on higher capital and operational complexity tied to autonomy milestones, manufacturing progress at Rivian and regulatory approvals in each launch city.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Uber Technologies Narrative

  • The partnership supports the narrative that autonomous partnerships and AI efficiencies can reshape Uber’s long term cost structure by securing a dedicated, electric robotaxi fleet aligned with its global AV push.
  • It also tests the narrative’s focus on an asset light model, because committing up to US$1.25b and volume purchases with Rivian increases exposure to AV hardware and execution risk if deployments are slower than planned.
  • The exclusivity of Rivian R2 robotaxis on Uber’s platform and the milestone based funding to 2031 are not fully reflected in high level AV commentary that mainly focuses on NVIDIA, Waymo or Zoox.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Uber Technologies to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Execution risk around Rivian meeting autonomous milestones, scaling production of the R2 SUV and achieving Level 4 readiness could affect how much of the planned US$1.25b is ultimately deployed and when vehicles join Uber’s fleet.
  • ⚠️ Regulatory, safety and legal scrutiny of robotaxis in cities like San Francisco and Miami, alongside existing class action and safety related cases, may influence rollout timing and operating costs compared with rivals such as Alphabet’s Waymo or Tesla.
  • 🎁 The exclusive access to up to 50,000 Rivian robotaxis gives Uber a differentiated all electric offering that can be marketed directly in its core app, which may support rider engagement versus Lyft and traditional taxi operators.
  • 🎁 Pairing Rivian’s vehicles with NVIDIA powered autonomy and Uber’s existing AV partners, including Motional, Zoox and Wayve, could increase ride supply on the platform, which may support trip density and utilisation if customer adoption is strong.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth tracking how Uber sequences the different AV partnerships, including where Rivian R2 robotaxis sit alongside NVIDIA powered fleets and Motional or Zoox vehicles in each city. Pay close attention to disclosed deployment timelines, city count, trip volumes and any commentary on unit economics for autonomous rides versus human driver trips. Updates on Rivian’s production status, validation of Level 4 capability and regulatory decisions in early launch markets such as San Francisco and Miami will be key signals for how quickly this exclusive robotaxi plan turns from announcement into a material part of Uber’s operations.

data.title: Is It Time To Reassess Zillow Group (ZG) After A 31.6% Year To Date Decline?
data.description:

  • The stock has moved 10.0% over the past 7 days and 2.0% over the past 30 days. Yet year to date the return sits at a 31.6% decline, with a 35.2% decline over 1 year and a 65.8% decline over 5 years.
  • Investors have been reacting to ongoing headlines around the US housing market, changing mortgage affordability and the role of online platforms in how buyers, sellers and agents connect. These themes have kept attention on how Zillow Group’s business model lines up with broader real estate trends and housing activity.

Zillow Group scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Approach 1: Zillow Group Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today’s dollars. It focuses on the cash the company might generate for shareholders rather than just current earnings or sales multiples.

For Zillow Group, the model starts with last twelve month free cash flow of about $137.7 million. Analysts provide explicit forecasts for several years, and Simply Wall St extends those projections further, using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. On this basis, Zillow’s free cash flow is projected at about $1.24b by 2030, with intermediate yearly estimates between 2026 and 2035 discounted back to present value.

Adding those discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of $97.81 per share. Against the current share price of about $44.89, the model implies a 54.1% discount, which indicates that the shares are trading well below this cash flow based estimate.

Result: UNDERVALUED

For companies where earnings can be uneven, the P/S ratio is often a cleaner way to think about value because it looks at what you are paying for each dollar of revenue, rather than profits that can swing around with accounting items or investment cycles.

Growth expectations and risk still matter, since investors usually accept a higher P/S multiple for businesses where they expect stronger revenue expansion and are comfortable with the risks, while slower or less certain growth tends to justify a lower multiple.

Zillow Group currently trades on a P/S of 4.17x. That is above both the Real Estate industry average of 2.48x and the peer average of 2.08x, which on the surface can make the shares look expensive compared with others in the space.

data.title: Knight Therapeutics Q4 Profit Tests Bullish Earnings Narrative After Yearlong Loss
data.description:

Knight Therapeutics FY 2025 earnings snapshot

Knight Therapeutics (TSX:GUD) has wrapped up FY 2025 with Q4 revenue of C$133.1 million and basic EPS of C$0.09, capping a year where the trailing twelve month figures show C$450.1 million in revenue and a basic EPS loss of C$0.05. Over recent quarters, revenue has moved from C$96.9 million in Q4 2024 to C$88.1 million in Q1 2025, C$107.4 million in Q2 2025, C$121.5 million in Q3 2025 and C$133.1 million in Q4 2025. Quarterly EPS has ranged between a loss of C$0.13 and a profit of C$0.11, leaving investors focused squarely on how margins are holding up across the year.

Revenue climbs to C$450.1 million over the year

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, Knight generated C$450.1 million of revenue, up from C$371.3 million at the end of FY 2024, while trailing twelve month net income moved from a profit of C$4.3 million to a loss of C$5.4 million.
  • Consensus narrative highlights strong organic revenue growth and a larger specialty portfolio as key positives, yet the shift from a trailing twelve month profit of C$4.3 million in Q4 2024 to a loss of C$5.4 million in Q4 2025 shows that higher sales have not yet translated into consistently positive earnings.

LTM EPS loss contrasts with bullish earnings forecasts

  • Even after a C$8.9 million net profit and C$0.09 EPS in Q4 2025, trailing twelve month EPS sits at a loss of C$0.05, compared with a trailing twelve month profit of C$0.04 per share a year earlier.
  • Bulls expect earnings to grow about 35.08% per year with profitability within three years, yet the current trailing twelve month loss of C$5.4 million and the data point that losses have grown at roughly 40.8% per year over five years mean the latest quarter has to be seen alongside a longer stretch of negative net margins.
    • This quarter’s C$8.9 million profit supports the bullish view that earnings can improve, but the trailing twelve month loss shows that one strong quarter has not yet reset the overall trend.
    • The bullish case leans on an analyst price target of C$8.03 versus today’s C$6.30 share price, so understanding how quickly trailing twelve month EPS moves from a C$0.05 loss toward positive territory is central to judging that view.

Some investors point to the strong Q4 profit and expected earnings growth as reasons to focus on the bullish case, while others are watching the trailing twelve month loss and five year loss trend just as closely. 🐂 Knight Therapeutics Bull Case

Valuation multiples balance optimism and past losses

  • The shares trade on a P/S of about 1.4x, compared with 0.9x for the Canadian pharmaceuticals industry and 1.2x for peers, while the DCF fair value in the dataset is C$85.16 and the analyst target used here is C$8.03 against a current price of C$6.30.
  • Bears argue that paying a higher P/S than peers is hard to justify while the company is still loss making, and the trailing twelve month loss of C$5.4 million alongside five year losses growing around 40.8% per year gives that concern real numbers behind it, even though models and analysts still see room between C$6.30 and C$8.03 for upside.
    • The higher P/S multiple fits with the idea that the market already prices in part of the expected 6.3% revenue growth and 35.08% earnings growth, which is what critics point to when they worry about downside if those forecasts are not met.
    • At the same time, the very large gap between the C$6.30 market price and the C$85.16 DCF fair value in the dataset shows why some investors see valuation support, even while bears focus on the recent period of negative net income.

data.title: GlobalFoundries Targets AI Data Centers With Silicon Photonics Push
data.description:
• GlobalFoundries (NasdaqGS:GFS) is expanding into silicon photonics, working with Flexcompute to link photonic device design tools directly to its manufacturing stack.
  • The collaboration focuses on optical interconnects for AI data centers, aiming to streamline the path from simulation to fabrication.
  • This move targets a growing market in data center optics while seeking to lower design and integration hurdles for customers.

GLOBALFOUNDRIES, trading at $42.47, is drawing attention in advanced semiconductor manufacturing as investors weigh its role in AI and data center infrastructure. The shares show a 15.2% gain year to date and 10.1% over the past year, while the 3-year return stands at a 37.8% decline, highlighting a mixed track record that some investors may see as an opportunity to reassess the story.

For readers tracking NasdaqGS:GFS, the silicon photonics initiative adds another angle beyond recent focus on equity offerings and shareholder transactions. The Flexcompute partnership could influence how customers think about designing on the GLOBALFOUNDRIES platform, particularly if closer integration between simulation and manufacturing shortens development cycles for AI-oriented optics.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for GLOBALFOUNDRIES by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on GLOBALFOUNDRIES.

We've flagged 0 risks for GLOBALFOUNDRIES. See which could impact your investment.

This Flexcompute collaboration speaks directly to how GLOBALFOUNDRIES is trying to carve out a role in AI data center plumbing, rather than competing head on with leading edge players like TSMC or Samsung. By placing its silicon photonics process stack inside PhotonForge, GLOBALFOUNDRIES is trying to make it easier for cloud and networking customers to move from photonic simulation to wafers that match foundry constraints. That tighter loop can matter when optical interconnects are dealing with copper limits beyond 200G per lane, where reach, power use, and bandwidth density become critical for AI clusters. For investors, the key question is whether this kind of design enablement makes GLOBALFOUNDRIES a preferred partner for optical module and switch vendors and helps the company capture more of the optical interconnect and silicon photonics spend in AI data centers.

How This Fits Into The GLOBALFOUNDRIES Narrative

  • The news lines up with the narrative that GLOBALFOUNDRIES is leaning into differentiated platforms for AI, communications, and automotive, using silicon photonics as another specialty technology alongside RF and FD SOI.
  • It also reinforces the capital intensity concern in the narrative, as expanding a full photonics stack and data center footprint in New York and Singapore can keep spending high if demand does not scale as expected.
  • The closer link between PhotonForge and GLOBALFOUNDRIES’ processes, and the focus on optical interconnects beyond copper limits, is not fully reflected in the narrative’s emphasis on automotive and mature nodes, so investors may want to factor in this AI optics angle separately.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for GLOBALFOUNDRIES to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ If optical networking growth or AI data center build outs slow, heavy investment in a silicon photonics stack could weigh on returns from GLOBALFOUNDRIES’ specialty platforms.
  • ⚠️ Larger foundries or integrated device makers could respond with their own tightly coupled design flows, which may dilute GLOBALFOUNDRIES’ differentiation in this niche.
  • 🎁 Tighter alignment between simulation and manufacturing can reduce design errors and re spins for customers, which may strengthen GLOBALFOUNDRIES’ position in high value optics programs.
  • 🎁 The focus on optical interconnects for AI workloads broadens GLOBALFOUNDRIES’ exposure beyond automotive and smart mobile and could support long term capacity use across multiple fabs.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, watch how many qualified GLOBALFOUNDRIES customers actually adopt the PhotonForge integration and whether it shows up in design wins for data center optics. Any commentary from management on silicon photonics wafer volumes across New York and Singapore sites will help you judge whether this collaboration is gaining traction. Also track how competitors such as TSMC, Intel, or Samsung talk about their own photonics ecosystems, since that will shape how differentiated GLOBALFOUNDRIES’ offering really is over the next few years.

data.title: PepsiCo Water Gains And Protein Snacks Reshape Growth And Risk Story
data.description:
• PepsiCo (NasdaqGS:PEP) reports it has met its 2025 pep+ water stewardship goals ahead of schedule, replenishing all water used at high risk sites.
  • The company has adopted global water stewardship standards across its facilities as part of its broader pep+ sustainability agenda.
  • PepsiCo has launched Good Warrior, a protein based meat stick brand, entering the protein snacks category.
  • The combination of early ESG goal completion and a new health focused product line could be relevant for how investors view PepsiCo’s risk profile and growth options.

PepsiCo operates a large global snacks and beverages business, so water use and resource efficiency are central to its long term operations. Meeting its pep+ water goals ahead of the 2025 target adds a concrete ESG data point that investors can weigh alongside more traditional financial metrics. At the same time, the move into protein snacks with Good Warrior shows PepsiCo responding to consumer interest in higher protein, convenience focused options.

For shareholders in NasdaqGS:PEP, these updates sit alongside the usual questions around margins, pricing power, and capital allocation. Over time, progress on water stewardship and expansion into protein based snacks could influence how resilient PepsiCo’s brand portfolio and supply chain appear, and how the company is positioned if health and sustainability keep shaping consumer spending.

For you as an investor, PepsiCo’s early completion of its pep+ water goals and the Good Warrior launch both speak directly to how the business model could evolve. Water stewardship targets sit close to core manufacturing risk, especially for a company that relies heavily on water-intensive beverages and snacks. Meeting those goals ahead of schedule may help reduce long-term operational and regulatory risk, particularly in high water-risk regions where supply interruptions or compliance costs can be material. At the same time, Good Warrior moves PepsiCo into protein-based snacks, a category that taps health-conscious, higher-protein demand already targeted by peers such as Mondelez and Nestlé. The key question is execution, including how well PepsiCo can scale distribution, secure quality protein sourcing, and avoid cannibalizing existing snacks while protecting margins. Together with other moves like reformulating legacy brands and acquiring better-for-you offerings, this news gives you more detail on how PepsiCo is trying to balance sustainability, product reformulation, and category expansion against cost pressures and brand complexity.

How This Fits Into The PepsiCo Narrative

  • The early water stewardship achievements support the narrative catalyst around sustainability and operational efficiency, which aims to make PepsiCo’s supply chain more resilient and cost-efficient over time.
  • The push into meat-based protein snacks could challenge the narrative if execution risks or portfolio complexity offset the benefits from health-oriented products and productivity programs.
  • The specific focus on water-risk mitigation and protein snacks may not be fully captured in the existing narrative, which centers more broadly on health-focused reformulation, international expansion, and technology-driven efficiency.

  • ⚠️ Expansion into meat-based snacks introduces new execution and reputational risks, including sourcing, food safety, and potential consumer pushback against processed meat at a time when some buyers are wary of ultra-processed foods.
  • 🎁 Trading at what some models see as a discount to estimated fair value sits alongside this push toward water stewardship and healthier-positioned products, which could support brand strength if consumer preferences continue to favor these attributes.
  • 🎁 Earnings are forecast in some analyses to grow at a mid single digit rate, and progress on ESG targets and health-focused innovation like Good Warrior may help support that type of growth if PepsiCo can convert these efforts into sustained demand.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth watching how PepsiCo integrates these water goals into everyday operations, including whether reduced water risk shows up in fewer disruptions or lower remediation costs at high-risk sites. On the product side, track how quickly Good Warrior gains shelf space, repeat purchases, and any extensions into other formats, especially relative to established protein-snack brands. It is also useful to watch how management balances spending on ESG projects and health-oriented launches with the already highlighted need to keep dividends and other cash uses on a sustainable footing. All of this will sit alongside upcoming earnings, where commentary on the pep+ program and the early performance of newer products can help you judge whether these initiatives are gaining traction.
